book_claims.mechanism: Plant protein source with calcium for bone health and ALA (alpha-linolenic acid), which can be partially converted to longer-chain omega-3s; legume consumption is a consistent dietary predictor of longevity across Blue Zone populations.
book_claims.text: Pinto beans are listed in the Longevity Diet as a calcium source (39 mg per 1/2 cup cooked, ~4% DV) and a plant omega-3 ALA source (0.17–0.24 g ALA per 3/4 cup cooked, alongside navy beans).

supplementary_research.source: PubMed — Bazzano et al., Archives of Internal Medicine (2001) — PMID 11176744
supplementary_research.finding: Legume consumption 4+ times per week associated with 22% lower risk of coronary heart disease compared to less than once per week in a prospective study of 9,632 adults, establishing legumes including pinto beans as longevity-protective foods.

supplementary_research.source: PubMed — Campos-Vega et al., Food Research International (2010) — PMID 21183845
supplementary_research.finding: Pinto beans are rich in polyphenols (kaempferol, quercetin, myricetin) and resistant starch; these compounds reduce postprandial glycemia, improve insulin sensitivity, and feed beneficial gut bacteria — mechanisms relevant to metabolic longevity.

supplementary_research.source: PubMed — Thompson et al., Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry (2009) — PMID 19572530
supplementary_research.finding: Pinto beans contain significant anthocyanin concentrations in their seed coat; dietary anthocyanins are associated with reduced cardiovascular risk and slower cognitive aging in longitudinal studies.

texture: holds shape for salads when al dente

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: PDCAAS ~0.70; limiting amino acid is methionine; complemented well by grains.
High soluble and insoluble fiber; soluble fraction (pectin, gums) lowers LDL cholesterol via bile acid sequestration.
One of the richest folate sources among legumes; important for DNA methylation, homocysteine control, and cell division.
Non-heme iron; absorption enhanced 2–3-fold with simultaneous vitamin C intake.

synergies.note: Classic complementary protein pair; pinto beans supply lysine (limiting in rice), rice supplies methionine (limiting in beans), together providing complete essential amino acid coverage.
Vitamin C from peppers markedly increases non-heme iron absorption from pinto beans, an important consideration in plant-based longevity diets.
Olive oil adds monounsaturated fats that improve the overall lipid profile of a legume-based meal and enhance absorption of fat-soluble carotenoids.
